task_completion.description: Open the specified rocket design file in OpenRocket.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenRocket application is visible with 'three_stage_low_power_rocket' loaded in the component tree or title bar.

task_completion.description: Inspect the ignition delays for the upper stages.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A configuration dialog (e.g., Motor Configuration tab) is open showing the ignition delay settings for the second and third stages.

task_completion.description: Configure or confirm the ignition delays for safe staging.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent sets or confirms the ignition delays for the second and third stages (e.g., setting them to 'Automatic' or a reasonable positive value for safe staging).

task_completion.description: Run a flight simulation to determine flight performance.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Flight Simulations tab is open in OpenRocket, and a simulation is run, displaying results such as apogee, velocity, or flight time.

task_completion.description: Save the updated rocket design to the original file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent saves the file (e.g., clicking File -> Save, or the absence of an unsaved changes indicator like an asterisk in the title bar after modifications).

task_completion.description: Create the staging optimization report file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing the file path '/home/ga/Documents/exports/staging_report.txt'.

task_completion.description: The report details the identified issues, new delay values, and resulting flight performance.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text in the report explicitly mentions the delay values used for the stages and includes flight performance metrics (e.g., apogee, max velocity) obtained from the simulation.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenRocket GUI to inspect/modify the rocket and run simulations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the OpenRocket graphical interface, rather than editing the .ork XML file directly in a text editor.

integrity.description: The flight performance metrics in the report match the simulation results.
integrity.visual_evidence: The numbers written in the report for flight performance (like apogee) match the results visible in the OpenRocket Flight Simulations tab.

integrity.description: The report contains actual simulation numbers and a coherent explanation.
integrity.visual_evidence: The report includes specific flight performance metrics and delay values, rather than just copy-pasting the exact wording of the task description.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The source file is located at /home/ga/Documents/rockets/three_stage_low_power_rocket.ork. The report must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/exports/staging_report.txt. Note: Due to a known issue in the task setup, the initial file might not actually have the corrupted delays (20.0s and 0.0s) injected as described in the prompt, and might still have the original correct 'automatic' or 0.0s delays. The agent should be evaluated on its attempt to inspect the delays, configure them for safe staging, run a simulation to get flight performance, and write the required report.
